about.title: W Studio Lab
about.quote: The tools have changed, but we've always been studying the same thing: how to let data speak for itself.
about.wslNote: WSL = W Studio Lab, also Windows Subsystem for Linux. A coincidence, and a metaphor.
about.description: Climate × City × AI — an independent tech lab for cross-domain scientific computing and AI engineering. Understanding the planet through data, serving cities through technology.

city.title: Digital City Lab
city.subtitle: Digitalization of Historic Buildings & Urban Space
city.description: Pudong Immovable Cultural Relics WebGIS Management System: spatial distribution of 241 relics, multi-dimensional filtering, protection level analysis, Web-GIS interaction. Data courtesy of SJTU-IRCAHC. Also covers urban planning and GIS research practices.

energy.title: Carbon & Renewables
energy.subtitle: WRF+AI Refined Solar Power Forecasting System
energy.description: Based on WRF regional weather model and AI bias correction technology, targeting distributed PV scenarios in Changzhou, Jiangsu, focusing on dual-rule assessment pain points to build a refined solar power forecasting system. Also covers carbon management platform architecture and dual-carbon informatization practices.
energy.comingSoonNote: The solar forecasting system is under development. Planned integration: WRF regional model + AI bias correction module. Upon completion, it will showcase accuracy comparisons, power curve forecasts, and dual-rule assessment analysis.

env.title: Environmental Monitoring
env.subtitle: Changzhou Real-time Air Quality Monitoring & Alert Platform
env.description: Based on CNEMC public APIs, real-time Changzhou air quality data with AQI, PM2.5, PM10, SO₂, NO₂, CO, O₃ multi-pollutant visualization and threshold alerts. Data auto-updates hourly with historical trend analysis.

works.title: AI Engineering Portfolio
works.subtitle: Full-stack AI Practice from Bidding to Customer Service
works.description: Desensitized showcase of professional works, limited to technical architecture and feature descriptions. Includes: BidMaster AI (AI-assisted bidding full-stack platform, TypeScript front/back-end + AI Agent); UTIC Service Center (AI-enhanced service portal, local knowledge base + Dify integration, 14,530 records with zero-latency retrieval).

weather.title: East China 7-Day Weather Analysis
weather.descPost: large model with NVIDIA Earth-2 inference framework, delivering 7-day, 6-variable East China regional weather forecasts. Inference time ~24 minutes, accuracy approaching ECMWF level.

figExplanations: The 500hPa geopotential height field is the core reference level for weather forecasting, representing the atmospheric flow pattern in the middle troposphere (~5500m). Dense blue contour regions correspond to troughs (cold air activity), while red-orange regions indicate ridges (high pressure control). Overlaid sea-level pressure contours (black) reveal surface weather system positions. The evolution from Mar29 to Apr04 clearly shows the eastward movement of the westerly trough and the subsequent high-pressure ridge control over East China — the key basis for forecasting the week's sunshine/rain pattern.

The 850hPa level near the top of the atmospheric boundary layer best represents low-level moisture transport direction. Wind barbs indicate wind direction and speed, with each full barb representing 10 knots (~5 m/s). Southwest wind zones indicate moisture transport corridors — a necessary condition for precipitation; northerly wind zones indicate cold air southward pathways. This chart is the key reference for determining precipitation location and intensity over East China.

The 700hPa relative humidity field is an important indicator of mid-level atmospheric moisture, directly linked to cloud development and precipitation potential. RH > 80% (dark areas) typically corresponds to cloud zones and potential precipitation; RH < 30% (light areas) indicates dry subsidence. The overlaid wind field shows mid-level steering flow direction, helping to determine weather system movement paths. Combined with 850hPa moisture transport, this provides a comprehensive assessment of precipitation probability distribution over East China.

The surface pressure field (sea-level pressure, SLP) is the most intuitive weather system indicator: low-pressure centers (L) correspond to cyclonic circulation and upward motion; high-pressure centers (H) correspond to anticyclones and fair weather. The 2m temperature (color fill) reflects near-surface thermal conditions, with warm/cold front positions identifiable through large temperature gradient zones. From Mar29 to Apr04, the eastward movement of the frontal system into the sea and the transition from cloudy/rainy to fair weather over East China can be clearly tracked.

The 10m wind field directly reflects surface wind conditions, with direct applications in wind energy assessment, offshore operations, and urban ventilation. Wind barbs are accurate to 5 knots, identifying strong wind zones (≥ Force 7). Combined with surface pressure gradients, the cause of strong winds can be determined (frontal passage, cyclone periphery, terrain acceleration, etc.). This chart is also an important reference for coastal disaster prevention, especially during the spring cold air season when accurate wind forecasts directly impact fishing and shipping safety.

Daily 24-hour accumulated precipitation is the most direct precipitation forecast product, with a color scale from light blue (light rain, < 10mm) to deep red (heavy rain, > 50mm). FuXi's precipitation forecast performance approaches ECMWF level, especially for large-scale precipitation systems (frontal rain, cyclonic rain). From the 7-day sequence, key precipitation event timing and locations can be identified, providing decision support for agriculture, water conservancy, and urban drainage.
